q_prof_intro.name: A Trade for Every Hand
q_prof_intro.text: Every soul in Eastbrook works a trade besides the sword, $N. There's ore veins in the rocks around the Copper Dig, southwest of town, so go swing a pick and bring me 5 chunks. Mine them yourself, mind; I'll know the difference.
q_prof_intro.completionText: See? Ore in your pack and callus on your hands. Keep at the mining, logging, and herb-picking as you travel the roads, and when you're back in town, mind the Town Focus board by the market and the crafting bench nearby. There's a fair trade waiting in all of it, if you want it.

q_wolves.name: Wolves at the Door
q_wolves.text: The forest wolves grow bold, snapping at travelers on the north road. Thin their numbers, $N. Slay 8 Forest Wolves and Eastbrook will breathe easier.
q_wolves.completionText: Fine work. The road feels safer already.

q_greyjaw.name: The Old Wolf
q_greyjaw.text: There is one wolf no trap has held: Old Greyjaw. He has taken three hounds and a stable boy's arm. He prowls the deep woods north of the wolf runs. Bring me his fang.
q_greyjaw.completionText: So the old devil is dead at last. The stable boy will sleep easier — and so will I.

q_boars.name: Bristly Boar Hides
q_boars.text: Boar hide makes the finest travel packs, and the meadows west of town are crawling with the beasts. Bring me 5 Bristly Boar Hides and I will make it worth your time.
q_boars.completionText: Ah, fine bristly hides! These will fetch a good price.

q_spiders.name: Sableweb Menace
q_spiders.text: The lurkers in the eastern woods spin a silk I need for my poultices — and they have grown far too numerous besides. Cull 6 Sableweb Lurkers and cut 4 silk glands from their bellies.
q_spiders.completionText: Ugh, still twitching. Perfect. Here, you've earned this.

q_murlocs.name: Trouble at the Lake
q_murlocs.text: Twenty years I have fished Mirror Lake, and never lost a net until those gurgling fish-men crawled out of the shallows. Drive the Mudfin back — slay 8 of them. And watch yourself: where there is one mudfin, there are five.
q_murlocs.completionText: Hah! That will teach them to mind their own mudholes.

q_mine.name: Rats in the Mine
q_mine.text: We struck a fine copper vein and then those burrowing vermin came boiling out of the hillside. My crew will not set foot in the dig until it is cleared. Put down 10 Deeprock Diggers.
q_mine.completionText: Ha! Back to work, lads! You have my thanks — and my coin.

q_bones.name: The Restless Dead
q_bones.text: The old ruin on the northwest hill was a chapel once, and its yard a resting place. Something has stirred the dead from their sleep. Grant them peace, $N — return 8 Restless Bones to the earth.
q_bones.completionText: May they rest now, and may the Light forgive whatever woke them.

q_supplies.name: Stolen Supplies
q_supplies.text: Those bandits hit my last wagon and made off with four crates of goods — tools, salt, good Eastbrook linen. The crates are stacked around their camp in the southeast hills. Steal them back for me, would you?
q_supplies.completionText: My crates! Barely a scratch on them. You are a wonder.

q_whispers.name: Whispers Below
q_whispers.text: You have laid the dead to rest, but they will not stay resting — something calls them back. Search the chapel ruin for any trace of the one doing the calling. If you find a sigil or seal, bring it to me untouched.
q_whispers.completionText: This sigil... it bears the mark of the Gravecallers, a sect I had prayed was extinct. This is worse than I feared, $N.

q_names_of_the_dead.name: The Names of the Dead
q_names_of_the_dead.text: If the Gravecallers raised our dead, I must know whose graves they robbed. The chapel sexton kept a burial ledger, and the wind has scattered its pages across the chapel yard. Gather 3 of them for me, $N — the dead deserve to be called by their names.
q_names_of_the_dead.completionText: These poor souls... and look here. Sexton Marrow — the chapel's own living caretaker — his grave the first disturbed. Morthen began with the very man who buried Eastbrook's dead.

q_silence_the_call.name: Silence the Call
q_silence_the_call.text: Every name in that ledger is a soul Morthen means to drag from the earth, and the chapel yard already crawls with those he has called. Return 12 Restless Bones to their graves, $N, before the Gravecaller's whisper swells into a chorus.
q_silence_the_call.completionText: The yard grows quieter — but the calling has not stopped. It rises from below now, $N. From the crypt itself.

q_rite.name: The Binding Rite
q_rite.text: The crypt beneath the chapel must be unsealed if we are to stop the Gravecaller — but only a binding rite will let the living pass. I need 4 lumps of Blessed Tallow — the mine's burrowers hoard tallow by the crate — and 6 Ghostly Essences from the restless dead.
q_rite.completionText: It is done. The way below stands open... and may the Light forgive me for opening it. Gather your strongest companions before you descend, $N. No one should face the Hollow alone.

q_hollow.name: Into the Hollow
q_hollow.text: Morthen the Gravecaller waits at the bottom of the Hollow Crypt, ringed by the elite dead he has raised. He is far beyond any one hero — take four companions, no fewer. End him, and the Vale's dead will finally sleep.
q_hollow.completionText: The whispering has stopped. You have done what the whole Vale could not, $N — the dead sleep, and Eastbrook owes you everything it has.

q_sexton.name: The Sexton's Bell
q_sexton.text: The ledger named him and the crypt holds him: Sexton Marrow, the chapel's caretaker, the first man Morthen raised — guarding his master's door in death as faithfully as he kept the chapel in life. Take four companions into the Hollow Crypt and grant the old sexton the rest he was robbed of, $N.
q_sexton.completionText: So Marrow is free at last. Ring no bell for him — he heard enough of them in life.

q_gravecallers_trail.name: The Gravecaller's Trail
q_gravecallers_trail.text: Morthen is dead, yet a question gnaws at me: a sect that hid for a century does not spend itself on one village chapel. He kept a grimoire — his rites, his correspondence. If anything of it survives, it lies in the vestry of the ruined chapel above the crypt. Search the ruin and bring me whatever remains of his writings, $N.
q_gravecallers_trail.completionText: Morthen wrote to a 'Fogbinder' in the northern fen. The sect is not dead, $N — it has merely been patient.

q_bandits.name: Bandits of the Vale
q_bandits.text: A pack of cutthroats has made camp in the southwest hills. They have robbed three wagons this week. Drive them out — slay 10 Vale Bandits.
q_bandits.completionText: Ten fewer knives in the dark. Take this — you have earned it.

q_ringleader.name: The Ringleader
q_ringleader.text: The bandits answer to one man: Gorrak the Ruthless. Cut off the head and the body will scatter. He skulks at the heart of their camp. End him, $N.
q_ringleader.completionText: Gorrak is dead? Then the Vale is free of his shadow. You have done Eastbrook a great service.

q_mogger.name: Mogger Must Fall
q_mogger.text: Mogger has split carts, flattened fences, and killed enough livestock to empty half the Vale. Do not face him alone. Take two strong companions into the eastern meadow and put the brute down for good.
q_mogger.completionText: Mogger dead at last. Eastbrook's fields are safer, and you leave the Vale with one more tale worth retelling.

q_archetype_acceptance.name: A Craft to Call Your Own
q_archetype_acceptance.text: Every artisan of Eastbrook eventually settles on one craft to call their own. Prove yourself with a single deed, $N, and declare your path.
q_archetype_acceptance.completionText: Your path is chosen; walk it well.

q_prof_make_amends.name: Making Amends
q_prof_make_amends.text: To set aside one craft for another, an artisan must first make amends for the path not walked, $N.
q_prof_make_amends.completionText: Amends made; a new path is open to you.

q_fenbridge_muster.name: Muster at Fenbridge
q_fenbridge_muster.text: Morthen's writings named a master in the northern marsh — a 'Fogbinder.' Now Warden Fenwick has sounded the muster horn at Fenbridge, and I do not believe in coincidence, $N. Take the causeway north, pull the muster order from the gatepost, and present it to the Warden.
q_fenbridge_muster.completionText: Aldric's seal, is it? Then you'll do. The fen has been swallowing my patrols whole, and I need every blade that floats.

q_prowlers.name: Teeth of the Fen
q_prowlers.text: The mire prowlers have learned what a supply mule sounds like, and now they hunt the causeway itself. Last week they dragged a courier into the reeds not fifty paces from this gate. Thin them out, $N — twelve dead prowlers ought to teach the rest fear.
q_prowlers.completionText: Twelve, and not a bite on you? The causeway breathes easier tonight.

q_prowler_pelts.name: Pelts for the Causeway
q_prowler_pelts.text: Every plank of that causeway rests on pilings wrapped in oiled prowler hide — the only thing the rot will not chew through. My stock is gone and the south spans are already sinking. Bring me 8 unspoiled pelts, $N, before we are all wading to Eastbrook.
q_prowler_pelts.completionText: Good thick pelts, these. The causeway will outlast the both of us now.

q_fen_supplies.name: The Lost Caravan
q_fen_supplies.text: A caravan out of Eastbrook went into the mist three days back and never rang the gate bell. The wreck is strewn the whole length of the causeway — crates, casks, the lot, sinking slow. Salvage 5 loads of goods before the marsh finishes the job.
q_fen_supplies.completionText: Waterlogged, but whole. Poor drivers... the fen keeps what it catches, $N. Remember that.

q_the_codfather.name: The Codfather
q_the_codfather.text: The Codfather isn't just a fish, $N, he's a cold-blooded killer. Old-timers swear he eats Mire Prowlers for breakfast, and even the Mirefen Widows won't spin their webs near the Deepfen Shallows out of sheer terror. He rules those waters. Grab a fishing pole, drag that old devil out of his waters, and I will admit you have joined the family.
q_the_codfather.completionText: By the damp saints... The Codfather himself. Look at those whiskers. Fenbridge will eat stories off this catch for a year, $N.

q_deepfen.name: The Deepfen Stirs
q_deepfen.text: The Deepfen mudfins kept to their shallows for twenty years. Now they swarm the east bank like flies on a carcass — and my wardens say they are dragging things up from the lake bed. Whatever has them stirred, I want it stopped. Cull 12 of the snappers.
q_deepfen.completionText: That will push them back to the mud for a while. But something set them digging, and I mean to learn what.

q_idols.name: Idols of the Deep
q_idols.text: Fenwick's wardens say the fish-men dredge idols from the lake bottom and clutch them like holy relics. If those idols are what I fear, I must see them with my own eyes. Take 5 from the Deepfen snappers — they will not part with them kindly.
q_idols.completionText: Gravecaller work — older than Morthen, older than me. The sect did not begin in Eastbrook, $N. It began here, and the lake has been keeping its secrets.

q_aldrics_fallen_star.name: Aldric's Fallen Star
q_aldrics_fallen_star.text: I saw a rock fall out of the western sky, $N. It struck the marsh wall and burst like a forge, far beyond the widow thicket. Go west, find what survived the explosion, and bring me anything that does not belong to this world.
q_aldrics_fallen_star.completionText: This is no weapon I know. Look at how the plates fold. It may be a rare piece of armor, if it can be worn at all. Take it and try it on, $N, but be careful.

q_deepfen_purge.name: Back to the Shallows
q_deepfen_purge.text: Aldric says those idols are cult-make — which means the mudfins are hauling the marsh's old evil up one armful at a time. I will not have it washing onto my causeway. Go back to the shallows and break the dredging for good: 14 more snappers.
q_deepfen_purge.completionText: Ruthless and thorough. If this marsh ever dries out, there's warden's work waiting for you.

q_widows.name: Silk and Venom
q_widows.text: Widow venom is the only thing that draws fen-rot from a wound — I bled a man of it just this morning. But the thicket west of the road has gone from nuisance to horror; the webs are taking deer whole now. Kill 10 widows and cut me 6 venom sacs, whole and unburst.
q_widows.completionText: Whole sacs, every one. You have steadier hands than half the surgeons in the south, $N.

q_broodmother.name: The Broodmother
q_broodmother.text: You have seen the webs — now ask yourself what spins cables thick as a man's wrist. The wardens call her the Broodmother, and her clutch hangs over Widow Thicket like a second canopy. Burn through 8 more widows and put the old mother down before that clutch opens.
q_broodmother.completionText: Dead? Truly dead? Then the thicket is just trees again. The Light bless your blade, $N.

q_drowned.name: The Drowned Dead
q_drowned.text: Travelers drowned on the causeway are walking out of the lakes, $N — still hung with the weeds they died in. This is no restless haunting. Drowning leaves no marks; it makes obedient corpses. Someone is filling this fen like a tithing box. Return 12 of the Drowned Dead to their rest.
q_drowned.completionText: Each one you fell is a stolen soul set free. But the one who drowned them is still pouring water.

q_drowned_censers.name: Censers from the Deep
q_drowned_censers.text: North of the widow lake stands a chapel that drowned with its congregation when the marsh rose. The dead there carry rusted censers — funerary ones, the kind swung at a Gravecaller rite. Gather 4 from the chapel yard and I will read what rite was sung over that water.
q_drowned_censers.completionText: As I feared. These censers burned grave-ash, not incense. Someone consecrated that chapel to the drowning — and the rite is signed 'Voss.'

q_no_rest.name: No Rest in the Reeds
q_no_rest.text: The rite on those censers binds the drowned to rise wherever the marsh touches them — and the marsh touches everything. There will be no rest in these reeds until the dead outnumber the living. We cannot unmake the rite yet, but we can empty it of soldiers. Lay 14 more of the Drowned Dead to rest.
q_no_rest.completionText: You give the dead more mercy than their masters ever did. Take this — you have more than earned it.

q_trolls.name: Mounds of the Mirefen
q_trolls.text: The Mirefen trolls have torn open the old barrow-mounds east of the far lake — burial mounds, $N, older than any kingdom of men. Whatever gold they think is down there, what they are letting OUT is worse. Drive them off the mounds: 12 trolls dead ought to do it.
q_trolls.completionText: Trolls don't dig without a reason. Someone told them where to dig — and I'd wager my gate it wears a grey robe.

q_troll_fetishes.name: Fetish and Bone
q_troll_fetishes.text: I crawled the troll mounds two nights past. Those fetishes they plant are not troll-craft — the knots are wrong, the bones are man-bones, and every one points at the open barrows like a signpost. Bring me 8 of them and I will prove to Fenwick who is really paying for this dig.
q_troll_fetishes.completionText: Same maker as the banners in the cult camp. The trolls are hired shovels, nothing more. Good work, $N.

q_grubjaw.name: The Glutton
q_grubjaw.text: There's one troll the others won't dig beside — Grubjaw, the Glutton. He ate my last two pack-mules, harness and all, and my insurance man drowned years ago. He prowls the far eastern mounds, $N. Bring me his tusk and I will outfit you proper.
q_grubjaw.completionText: That tusk is long as my forearm! The mules are avenged, and Fenbridge owes you a round.

q_cult_camp.name: Robes in the Reeds
q_cult_camp.text: There — north past the third lake, where the mist never lifts. Grey robes, grey banners: Gravecallers, camped in the open like they already own the fen. They have stopped hiding, $N, which means they think they have already won. Prove them wrong. Cut down 12 of their cultists.
q_cult_camp.completionText: Twelve robes face-down in the mud. Now they know the fen watches back.

q_summoners.name: Stopping the Summoning
q_summoners.text: Maren's reports name summoners among the cultists — voices that call the drowned up out of the water like hounds to a whistle. Their ciphers will spell out the chain of command. Silence 8 summoners and bring me 4 of their ciphers.
q_summoners.completionText: Every cipher is countersigned 'Deacon Voss' — and addressed onward to a 'Fogbinder' in the Bastion. Morthen's master, $N. We have found him.

q_deacon.name: The Deacon of the Mire
q_deacon.text: So a deacon of the Gravecallers stands at the heart of that camp, singing my drowned wardens up out of the lakes to serve him. His hymn ends today. Take the camp road north, $N, and put Deacon Voss in the ground — deep enough that nobody sings HIM back up.
q_deacon.completionText: Voss is dead and the mist over the camp is already thinning. You have broken their voice in the fen — now only the Bastion remains.

q_bastion_door.name: The Sunken Bastion
q_bastion_door.text: The Sunken Bastion — a knight's hold that drowned in the fen a century ago — is where Voss's letters point, and where this Fogbinder sings his drowning hymns. The cult has warded its door with grave-stones. Bring me one of the ward stones, $N, and I will unweave the seal.
q_bastion_door.completionText: The ward parts like rotten rope. The door stands open... and the dark below it is listening.

q_olen.name: The Knight-Commander's Shame
q_olen.text: Knight-Commander Olen held the Bastion when it sank — drowned at his post rather than abandon it. Every warden learns his name with pride. Now the Fogbinder has raised him as a puppet to guard the very door he died defending. That shame ends, $N. Take four companions below and grant Olen the rest he earned.
q_olen.completionText: Then his watch is finally over. I'll see his name cut into the gate myself. Thank you, $N.

q_mistcaller.name: The Fogbinder
q_mistcaller.text: At the bottom of the Bastion waits Vael the Fogbinder — Morthen's master, Voss's master, the voice that has drowned a hundred travelers to raise itself an army. He is far beyond any one hero: take four companions, no fewer. End him, $N, and the fen's dead may finally lie still.
q_mistcaller.completionText: Vael is dead, and the mist is lifting for the first time in years. But Maren heard his last words, and they freeze my blood: 'The Wyrm stirs beneath the peaks.' The sect serves something older than we ever guessed, $N. Rest while you can — the mountains are next.

q_highwatch_summons.name: The Watch on the Peaks
q_highwatch_summons.text: Vael's last words have not left me, $N: the Wyrm stirs beneath the peaks. Captain Thessaly commands the wall at Highwatch, at the head of the mountain road north. A summons stands posted at her gate — take it up, and tell her Brother Aldric is climbing the mountain behind you.
q_highwatch_summons.completionText: Aldric's word reaches far. If the priest of the Vale is climbing the mountain himself, then it is as bad as I feared. Welcome to Highwatch, $N.

q_stalkers.name: Stalkers on the Ridge
q_stalkers.text: The ridge cats have come down from the high snows hungry, and my patrols bleed for it. Every stalker you put down is a soldier I keep on the wall. Thin them, $N — twelve, to start.
q_stalkers.completionText: Twelve fewer shadows on the ridge. The patrols will breathe easier tonight.

q_stalker_pelts.name: Winter Is Coming to Highwatch
q_stalker_pelts.text: Winter on this mountain does not knock, $N — it kicks the door in. Eight ridge stalker pelts will line enough cloaks to see the wall through the first snows. The beasts prowl the ridges flanking the road south.
q_stalker_pelts.completionText: Thick as my arm, these. The watch will not freeze this year — take these treads for your trouble.

q_stalkers_return.name: The Stalkers Return
q_stalkers_return.text: Twelve dead, and the ridge crawls thicker than the day you started, $N. Beasts do not throw themselves at a wall out of hunger. Something on the high ridge is pushing them down, and until I know what, the culling does not stop. Fourteen more.
q_stalkers_return.completionText: Fourteen more, and still my patrols count fresh tracks by morning. My scout came back from the high ridge white as the snowline: prints the size of a shield, she says, and old kills no stalker would leave. Whatever walks up there is no ordinary cat.

q_stalker_cloaks.name: Cloaks for the Watch
q_stalker_cloaks.text: Eight pelts lined the officers' cloaks, and now every soldier on the wall wants the same, $N. They are right to want it: winter takes fingers first and apologies never. Ten more pelts from the ridges south of the gate, and the whole watch sleeps warm.
q_stalker_cloaks.completionText: Ten good pelts, thick as any I have... no, look at these, $N. Torn, half of them, and by no blade or spear. Claw marks wide as my hand, right through the winter coat. Something on that ridge is savaging its own kind.

q_old_cragmaw.name: Old Cragmaw
q_old_cragmaw.text: The mountain folk put a name to the prints my scout found: Old Cragmaw, a scar-pelted tyrant of a cat that has outlived three generations of its own pack. It is the reason the stalkers flood my road, $N. Its den sits on the western ridge above the road south. Bring a friend, and put the old devil down.
q_old_cragmaw.completionText: Down at last. The mountain folk swore that cat would outlive the wall itself. The stalkers will keep to their high snows now, $N, and my patrols will walk the road without bleeding for it. The whole ridge is quieter for your work.

q_kobold_tunnels.name: Deeprock Trouble
q_kobold_tunnels.text: The tunnelers at Deeprock Burrows are digging deeper than any pit-rat has business digging — straight down, as if something were calling them. Their tunnels run beneath our wall, $N. Collapse the matter: kill twelve Deeprock Tunnelers.
q_kobold_tunnels.completionText: Straight down, every shaft of it — burrowers do not dig like that on their own. I must consult my books.

q_glowing_wax.name: Strange Wax
q_glowing_wax.text: Caddis showed me a lump of wax taken off one of those tunnelers — it glows, $N, and it is warm as a heartbeat. He wants more for study, and I want it off my requisition list. Bring back six lumps of the glowing wax.
q_glowing_wax.completionText: Still warm. The Loremaster says the glow matches no flame he knows of. I say it is mountain trouble, and I say it kindly.

q_ogre_edges.name: Ogres at the Foothills
q_ogre_edges.text: The Thornpeak clans never come this far east — yet here they are, camped in the eastern foothills with war paint on. Somebody is paying them, $N, and ogres do not take promises. Cut twelve of them down while I find out who holds the purse.
q_ogre_edges.completionText: Twelve down, and still they are not pulling back. Whoever bought them paid in something heavier than gold.

q_ogre_totems.name: Totems of War
q_ogre_totems.text: Around the war-camp the ogres have raised totems — crude things of hide and skull, but they mark a muster, not a raid. Tear down six of them and bring them to me. Mind the crushers on the perimeter, $N.
q_ogre_totems.completionText: Skull, hide... and look here — wyrm-scale bindings. These totems were gifts, $N. The cult is arming the clans.

q_ogre_bounty.name: The Captain's Bounty
q_ogre_bounty.text: Maren's totems tell me all I need to know: the clans are bought, and my wall is their first errand. I will not wait for them to muster. Fourteen more Thornpeak Ogres, $N — and I will pay bounty on every one.
q_ogre_bounty.completionText: Bounty paid in full. The foothills are quieter — now we deal with the ones doing the buying.

q_crushers.name: Break the War-Camp
q_crushers.text: Drogmar's war-camp squats in the eastern crags, and his crushers are the spine of it — each one worth three of my soldiers. Take companions; this is no errand for one blade. Break ten crushers and the warlord's muster breaks with them.
q_crushers.completionText: Ten crushers down. The war-camp is a body without a spine — time to take the head.

q_drogmar.name: Warlord Drogmar
q_drogmar.text: Warlord Drogmar took the Wyrmcult's coin and swore the clans to the mountain's waking. He is the hammer they mean to swing at my wall — and when he slams the ground, $N, do not be standing near him. Take your companions into the war-camp and end him, for Highwatch.
q_drogmar.completionText: Drogmar, dead in his own camp. The clans will scatter to the high passes — you have bought my wall a winter, $N.

q_elementals.name: The Mountain Wakes
q_elementals.text: Stormcrag has stood silent a thousand years, and now the very stones of it get up and walk. Elementals do not simply wake, $N — something beneath this mountain is turning in its sleep. Put twelve of them down so I may study what remains.
q_elementals.completionText: The fragments hum like struck bells. The mountain is not angry, $N... it is being disturbed.

q_shard_cores.name: Cores of the Storm
q_shard_cores.text: At each elemental's heart sits a storm core — a knot of lightning bound in stone. Six of them, set side by side, will tell me where the disturbance is centered. I suspect I already know, $N, and I dearly hope that I am wrong.
q_shard_cores.completionText: Each core leans the same way, like iron filings to a lodestone. They point south, $N. To the Sanctum.

q_kazzix.name: The Shardlord
q_kazzix.text: Among the elementals one burns brighter than the rest: Shardlord Kazzix, a storm given shoulders. Its heartshard would anchor every reading I have taken — if you can wrench it from the thing. It walks the far crags west of Stormcrag, beyond the second camp.
q_kazzix.completionText: The heartshard! Still crackling — magnificent. Take these leggings; I sized them off a guess and a prayer.

q_zealots.name: Chants on the Wind
q_zealots.text: When the wind comes off the southern peaks, $N, it carries chanting. The Wyrmcult no longer hides — they have raised tents below the Sanctum and they sing to what sleeps beneath it. Silence twelve zealots. Every voice stilled buys the mountain another night of sleep.
q_zealots.completionText: The wind is quieter. But what troubles me is not the chanting, $N — it is that something may be chanting back.

q_cult_orders.name: Orders from Below
q_cult_orders.text: The zealots move with purpose now — watches set, supplies counted, like soldiers before a siege. Cultists who organize are cultists taking orders, $N. Kill eight more and bring me four sets of their written orders. I would know the hand that commands them.
q_cult_orders.completionText: This script... I last saw its like in Morthen's grimoire, in Eastbrook. The same hand has guided every grave we have fought over, $N.

q_necromancers.name: The Phylactery Ring
q_necromancers.text: The orders speak of a "ring of phylacteries" — soul-vessels, $N, set about the Sanctum to feed it. The cult's necromancers carry them like holy relics. Kill eight necromancers and bring me three phylacteries unbroken. I must know what souls they hold.
q_necromancers.completionText: Light forgive us. These hold the dead of the Vale and the fen — every corpse the Gravecallers ever raised, harvested. They were never building an army, $N. They were gathering a tithe.

q_revenants.name: The Revenant Fields
q_revenants.text: East of the Sanctum road lies an old battlefield — the vanguard of the last army that tried to take this mountain, two hundred years buried. The cult has called them up, bones in rusted plate. Put twelve revenants back in the ground, $N.
q_revenants.completionText: They were soldiers once, like mine. Whatever called them up has no respect for the dead — or a use for them I do not care to learn.

q_revenant_vanguard.name: Bones of the Vanguard
q_revenant_vanguard.text: The revenants are forming ranks, $N — true ranks, shield-lines and columns, drilling with no drummer. They are being mustered for the Sanctum gate. Break fourteen more before that march begins, and Highwatch will owe you its best steel.
q_revenant_vanguard.completionText: The fields lie still again. Take this — it was made for the defenders of the wall, and no one has earned it more.

q_wyrm_sigils.name: Sigils of the Wyrm
q_wyrm_sigils.text: It is time you knew the whole of it, $N. The Gravecallers serve Korzul the Gravewyrm — an ancient dragon sealed beneath this mountain — and every soul they have stolen since Eastbrook is a tithe poured into its waking. On the Sanctum Approach the cult has laid sigils to thin the seal. Bring me three; I would read the rite they are working.
q_wyrm_sigils.completionText: Yes... a waking-litany, generations in the writing. They are close, $N. Closer than I dared fear.

q_breaking_the_seal.name: Breaking the Seal
q_breaking_the_seal.text: The seal on the Sanctum was wrought with mountain-fire, and only mountain-fire will let us pass without tearing it wide open. The stormcrag elementals carry embers of that first forging in their cores. Bring me five Blessed Embers, $N — for if the cult opens that gate first, they will not be careful, and the Wyrm will not wake gently.
q_breaking_the_seal.completionText: They burn blue and clean — the mountain remembers its old oath. With these I can unbind the gate for us alone.

q_voice_below.name: The Voice Below
q_voice_below.text: Last night the whole cult camp knelt at once, $N — every zealot, every necromancer, all facing the Sanctum. Korzul speaks to them in their sleep now; Vael heard the same voice in the fen, and Morthen before him. Cut the congregation down — ten zealots, six necromancers — before that voice has hands enough to pull the gate open itself.
q_voice_below.completionText: The kneeling has stopped. We have not silenced the voice, $N — only thinned its choir. It must be enough.

q_sanctum_gate.name: The Sanctum Gate
q_sanctum_gate.text: This is the last threshold, $N. The gate of the Gravewyrm Sanctum was locked with a keystone, and the cult shattered it into shards rather than see it turned against them. The shards lie scattered in the gate plaza, under the eyes of the boneclad dead. Bring me three, and I will open the way the Light intended — quietly.
q_sanctum_gate.completionText: The shards sit true... and the gate knows its key. The way below stands open, $N. Gather the strongest companions you can find — what comes next, no one should face alone.

q_korgath.name: The Bound Guardian
q_korgath.text: My last sweep of the Sanctum's mouth found chains, $N — chains thick as a ship's mast, and something ogre-shaped straining inside them. The cult bound a champion at the threshold: Korgath, fed on rage for longer than either of us has been alive. Take four companions and put him down — and when the chains come off, do not let him corner you.
q_korgath.completionText: Korgath, broken at last. Even his chains deserved a kinder end than that. The wraps are yours — wear them past the threshold he kept.

q_velkhar.name: The Grand Necromancer
q_velkhar.text: Every thread we have followed — Morthen, Vael, the phylacteries — was spun by one hand: Grand Necromancer Velkhar, first of the Gravecallers, keeper of the waking rite. He stands in the ritual vault below, pouring two lands' worth of stolen souls into the Wyrm. End him, $N, and the tithe ends with him.
q_velkhar.completionText: Velkhar is dead, and the rite is headless. But you felt it down there, did you not? The souls are already spent — the Wyrm is no longer asleep.

q_gravewyrm.name: Korzul the Gravewyrm
q_gravewyrm.text: There is no rite left to stop, $N — only the Wyrm itself, half-woken in its hollow, gorged on the dead of the Vale and the fen. If it rises, the wall, the marsh, Eastbrook — everything we have defended falls in a single night. Take your companions into the Wyrm's Hollow and finish what we began in a chapel yard so long ago. The Light has carried you this far; carry it the rest of the way.
q_gravewyrm.completionText: It is over. The dead of three lands may rest, the mountain sleeps unhaunted — and it is your name, $N, that every bell from here to Eastbrook rings tonight.

q_nythraxis_restless_dead.name: Unrest in the Bonefields
q_nythraxis_restless_dead.text: Something has changed in Thornpeak Heights, $N. The dead no longer wander aimlessly. They gather and march through the northern bonefields beyond Highwatch, where the old battlefield meets the cliff road. Go there, investigate the unrest among the Boneclad Revenants, and bring back any proof of what is driving them.
q_nythraxis_restless_dead.completionText: The same mark appears on every shard... a crown. I have seen this before, cut into old graves no Eastbrook record remembers.

q_nythraxis_graves.name: Graves of the Forgotten
q_nythraxis_graves.text: I have seen these marks before, on three old graves around the northern battlefield. Captain Aldren lies on the eastern rise, High Priest Malric near the central broken road, and Royal Assassin Voss by the western cliff. Touch each grave and listen, $N. The dead may remember what the living forgot.
q_nythraxis_graves.completionText: Aldren remained loyal, Malric refused to accept death, and Voss saw the danger before anyone else. All three served the same forgotten king.

objectives.label: Vision at the Grave of Captain Aldren
Vision at the Grave of High Priest Malric
Vision at the Grave of Royal Assassin Voss

q_nythraxis_sealed_crypt.name: The Abandoned Crypt
q_nythraxis_sealed_crypt.text: The visions point to the abandoned crypt in the western cliff. There is an old legend that the crypt housed a king. Perhaps Thornpeak sealed him below after Malric's ritual twisted him into something deathless. Enter the crypt and see what remains inside.
q_nythraxis_sealed_crypt.completionText: The keystone halves fit together, and Voss's diary names what they sealed: the signet of King Nythraxis. If the diary is true, that signet is the key to his tomb.

q_nythraxis_bound_guardian.name: The Bound Guardian
q_nythraxis_bound_guardian.text: Voss wrote that the survivors sealed the King's Signet behind an ancient guardian, so no one could reach the tomb of Nythraxis by accident or ambition. Take the Crypt Keystone to the ritual circle on the flat ground north-west of the abandoned crypt and north-east of High Priest Malric's grave. Use it there, break the guardian, and bring back the signet.
q_nythraxis_bound_guardian.completionText: The three relics tell the same story: Aldren fought to defend his king, Malric broke the boundary of death, and Voss tried to stop what followed. The seal is weakening, and this signet is the key to Nythraxis's tomb. You are now attuned to enter The Crypt of Nythraxis. Return to the abandoned crypt, unlock the royal door, and face Nythraxis before the old king's rage spills beyond Thornpeak.

objectives.label: Crypt Keystone used at the ritual circle

q_nythraxis_scourges_end.name: Scourge's End
q_nythraxis_scourges_end.text: The signet has opened the way, $N, but an open tomb is not a victory. Nythraxis was a king once, and the ruin beneath Thornpeak is still bound to his will. Enter the crypt with allies you trust. Break the deathless crown before its command reaches the battlefield above.
q_nythraxis_scourges_end.completionText: Then the crown is silent at last. Thornpeak will still carry its dead, but no king below it will call them to war again. You have ended what Aldren, Malric, and Voss could only contain.

q_glimmermere_light.name: Light on the Water
q_glimmermere_light.text: Look there, $N — under the surface, a stair of pale stone running down into the dark, and a gate of cold light at the head of it. The old wardens scratched warnings into the shore-rocks before the water took them. Take a rubbing of one for me; I would read what they feared before we go any closer.
q_glimmermere_light.completionText: A waking-prayer... to something they called the Drowned Moon. And below it, in a steadier hand: "It only sleeps." The water has been listening a long time, $N.

q_tarn_waders.name: What the Tarn Gives Up
q_tarn_waders.text: Since the gate opened, things climb out of the mere at dusk — bloated, pale, finned where hands ought to be. Glimmermere Waders, the old rubbings name them. They drag anything living back down with them. Cull ten before they thin my watch to nothing.
q_tarn_waders.completionText: Ten back in the water. They feel no cold, $N, and no fear — only the pull of that gate. Whatever sings to them, it sings loud.

q_drowned_choir.name: The Drowned Choir
q_drowned_choir.text: The waders do not act alone. Among them walk the Drowned Votaries — the cult that sank with the temple, still in their rotted vestments, still singing the prayer from the shore-rocks. Silence eight of them, and bring me six of the offerings they carry. I would know what they mean to give their goddess.
q_drowned_choir.completionText: Pearls, knuckle-bones, a child's carved fish... grave-gifts, $N. They are not raising the dead. They are dressing them, the way you dress a body for burial. The temple is a tomb that refuses to close.

q_palecoil.name: Sethrael the Palecoil
q_palecoil.text: One shape in the mere is no drowned man. A serpent the colour of bone glides the deep shelf where the stair begins — Sethrael, the rubbings call it, the Palecoil, the moon's own watch-beast. While it guards that water, no one reaches the gate alive. Go down to the shelf and kill it, $N. Take its heartscale so I know the deed is done.
q_palecoil.completionText: Cold as the bottom of the world, and still it twitches. The shelf is clear, $N — the stair to the gate stands open. I almost wish it did not.

q_silence_the_choir.name: Silence the Choir
q_silence_the_choir.text: The singing comes from below the gate now, and one voice leads it: Choirmother Selthe, who first taught the cult to drown without dying. While she keeps the prayer, the temple will never sleep — and the mere will never stop giving up its dead. Take companions through the gate and end her. This is no errand for a lone blade, $N.
q_silence_the_choir.completionText: The prayer falters... and for the first time in thirty nights, the mere is quiet. But quiet is not the same as ended. Selthe was only the choir. Something below it still listens.

q_drowned_moon.name: The Drowned Moon
q_drowned_moon.text: I have read the last of the rubbings, $N, and I understand now what the cult drowned themselves to keep asleep. Ysolei — the Drowned Moon made flesh — coils on the altar at the temple's heart, and the stolen warmth of every life the mere took is pouring into her waking. When the moon stands full she rises, and the water rises with her — the tarn, the wall, the whole mountain under it. Gather the strongest you can find and put her back to sleep. For good, this time.
q_drowned_moon.completionText: The altar is dark, the water is still, and the moon over the tarn is only the moon. You drowned a goddess tonight, $N — and the mountain will never know how close it came. Let the wardens of the shore-rocks rest easy at last.

objectives.label: Ysolei, Avatar of the Drowned Moon, slain
